sections.markdown:
- Activity: Morning Run
- Started: 2026-03-01 09:11
- Distance: 9.6 km
- Moving time: 1:05:43
- Elapsed time: 1:07:35
- Elevation gain: 41 m
- Average heart rate: 171.8 bpm
- Max heart rate: 200 bpm
- Average watts: 182.1 W
- Average speed: 8.7 km/h

- Apple Health recorded `242` during-run heart-rate samples. Average HR was `169.5 bpm`, ranging from `119.0` to `198.5 bpm`.
- First-half average HR was `166.3 bpm` and second-half average HR was `174.3 bpm` (+8.0 bpm), which shows a modest late rise in aerobic cost.

- Apple Health cadence averaged `157.8 spm`, with a working range of `111.3` to `172.0 spm` after excluding `7` start-stop outliers below `100 spm`.
- First-half cadence averaged `161.1 spm` and second-half cadence averaged `153.1 spm` (-8.0 spm), which suggests some late turnover fade.

- Strava moving pace averaged `6:53 /km` across `9.551 km` in `1:05:43` moving time.
- Estimated first-half moving pace was `6:42 /km` and second-half pace was `7:04 /km` (+21 s/km), so pace faded meaningfully in the back half.

- Strava summary HR came in at `171.8 bpm` average and `200 bpm` max.
- Distance-split HR averaged `165.9 bpm` in the first half and `177.5 bpm` in the second (+11.6 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Moving time was `1:05:43` out of `1:07:35` elapsed (97.2%), which shows only minor interruption cost.
- The downsampled moving stream shows `8` stop segments and `1.0%` stationary samples, so the run was mostly continuous with a few interruptions.

- Strava logged about `51.8 m` of climbing, with altitude moving between roughly `4.4 m` and `19.6 m`.
- Estimated climbing was `26.2 m` in the first half and `25.6 m` in the second, so the climbing load was split fairly evenly.
